project.name: C3 one-directional reverse-endpoint fixture
project.description: Purpose-built plan-18 fixture for the C3 reverse-endpoint contract. Two NAT-side peers dial one public hub; neither has a reverse edge. 'c3-endpoint' sets public_endpoints WHILE has_public_ip=false (the C3 case) and MUST get a populated reverse Endpoint via the plan-8 HasPublicIP normalization; 'c3-natpeer' is genuinely unreachable (no public_endpoints) and MUST get an EMPTY reverse Endpoint (correct one-directional). The kernel run proves the contrast is real and both tunnels still form (the peers dial in).

public_endpoints.note: reachable endpoint set WHILE has_public_ip=false — the C3 case

edges.notes: c3-endpoint dials the hub; no reverse edge — the reverse Endpoint comes only from the fallback
c3-natpeer dials the hub; no reverse edge and no public_endpoints — reverse Endpoint stays empty (correct)
